findings.requirement: Lawful basis for processing personal data (Art. 6)
findings.issue: No explicit consent mechanism or lawful basis documented for collecting and storing user email and password.
findings.remediation: Add explicit consent checkbox during registration and log consent timestamp; document lawful basis (e.g., 'performance of contract') in privacy notice.

findings.issue: User model stores email and hashed password, but no indication of whether additional fields (e.g., name, phone) are optional or enforced — risk of over-collection.
findings.remediation: Enforce strict schema validation to collect only email and password at registration; defer optional fields until profile update with separate consent.

findings.issue: No 'delete account' or data deletion endpoint implemented; user data cannot be fully erased on request.
findings.remediation: Implement DELETE /api/v1/users/me with cascading deletion of todos and token records, and confirm irreversible action.

findings.issue: JWT validation lacks token revocation check (e.g., against a denylist or short-lived tokens only); stale tokens may remain valid post-password reset or logout.
findings.remediation: Introduce token denylist (e.g., Redis-stored jti blacklist) or adopt rotating refresh tokens with strict binding and short access token TTL (<15 min).

findings.requirement: CC7.2 – Encryption of sensitive data in transit and at rest
findings.issue: No evidence of PostgreSQL-level encryption (TDE) or application-layer encryption for PII; passwords are hashed but email is stored in plaintext.
findings.remediation: Enable PostgreSQL SSL/TLS in transit; encrypt email at rest using deterministic AEAD (e.g., pgcrypto + application-managed keys) or enforce column-level encryption.

action_items.action: Add explicit consent capture and logging during user registration
Implement account deletion endpoint with full cascade and confirmation
Integrate JWT denylist or rotate short-lived tokens with strict binding
Enable TLS for PostgreSQL and add email encryption at rest
Define and document data retention policy (e.g., 30 days after account deletion)
